canonical_mini_examples.snippet:
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/layouts/stack-layout-params.h>
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/layouts/stack-layout.h>
#include <dali/integration-api/debug.h>

using namespace Dali;
using namespace Dali::Ui;

/**
 * AnimatedImageView sample.
 *
 * Demonstrates:
 *   - Play / Pause / Stop / JumpToFrame
 *   - Change Image  (cycles through WebP, GIF×2, URL array)
 *   - LoopCount  (infinite / 3 / 1)
 *   - FrameSpeedFactor  (0.5× / 1.0× / 2.0×)
 *   - StopBehavior  (CURRENT / FIRST / LAST)
 *   - SetFrameDelay  (200ms / 500ms / 1000ms / 2000ms)
 *   - GetPlayState / GetCurrentFrame / GetTotalFrame  (logged on each button press)
 *   - AnimationFinishedSignal
 *
 * Image sources:
 *   0. dog-anim.webp          (animated WebP)
 *   1. dali-logo-anim.gif     (animated GIF)
 *   2. animatedLoading.gif    (animated GIF)
canonical_mini_examples.usage_note: Short verified evidence snippet. Prefer the documented API contract over copying this verbatim.
Short verified evidence snippet. Prefer the documented API contract over copying this verbatim.

symbols.brief: AnimatedImageView is a View for displaying animated image resources such as GIF files.
Dali::Animation can be used to animate the properties of any number of objects, typically Actors.
This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
Move constructor.
Creates an uninitialized Animation; this can be initialized with Animation::New().
An interface identifying the root object associated with a running application.
An Application class object should be created by every application that wishes to use Dali.
Move constructor.
Copy Constructor.

symbols.brief: Enumeration for what state the animation is in.
The animation is paused.
The animation is playing.
Animation has stopped.
Enumeration for what to do when the animation is stopped.
When the animation is stopped, the current frame is shown.
When the animation is stopped, the first frame is shown.
When the animation is stopped, the last frame is shown.
FittingMode configures how the image fits within the defined bounds.
The visual should keep original size of image.

members.name: DownCast

members.brief: Jumps to the specified frame number.
members.name: JumpToFrame

#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/layouts/stack-layout-params.h>
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/layouts/stack-layout.h>
#include <dali/integration-api/debug.h>

using namespace Dali;
using namespace Dali::Ui;

/**
 * AnimatedImageView sample.
 *
 * Demonstrates:
 *   - Play / Pause / Stop / JumpToFrame
 *   - Change Image  (cycles through WebP, GIF×2, URL array)
 *   - LoopCount  (infinite / 3 / 1)
 *   - FrameSpeedFactor  (0.5× / 1.0× / 2.0×)
 *   - StopBehavior  (CURRENT / FIRST / LAST)
 *   - SetFrameDelay  (200ms / 500ms / 1000ms / 2000ms)
 *   - GetPlayState / GetCurrentFrame / GetTotalFrame  (logged on each button press)

 * Image sources:
 *   0. dog-anim.webp          (animated WebP)
 *   1. dali-logo-anim.gif     (animated GIF)
 *   2. animatedLoading.gif    (animated GIF)

section_policy.guidance: After the title, overview, and table of contents, choose feature-specific topic sections. Do not use a fixed section template for every feature. Sections should explain meaningful usage themes rather than list APIs mechanically.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

members.brief: The URL of the alpha mask image.

members.name: CACHE_SIZE

members.name: CROP_TO_MASK

members.brief: The desired decoding height hint in pixels. 0 means no constraint.

members.name: FITTING_MODE

members.brief: Playback speed multiplier. 1.0 is normal speed.
members.name: FRAME_SPEED_FACTOR

members.name: IMAGE

members.name: IMAGE_COLOR

members.name: IMAGE_LOAD_WITH_VIEW_SIZE

members.name: MASKING_MODE

members.brief: The normalized sub-region of the image to display.
members.name: PIXEL_AREA

members.brief: The URL of the placeholder image shown while loading.
members.name: PLACEHOLDER_IMAGE

members.brief: Whether to apply pre-multiplied alpha to the rendered output.

members.name: SAMPLING_MODE

members.brief: The behavior when the animation is stopped.
members.name: STOP_BEHAVIOR

members.brief: Whether to load the image synchronously on the event thread.
members.name: SYNCHRONOUS_LOADING
